JavaScript Object Notation for Linked Data (JSON-LD)

Media Type: application/xml is recommended as of RFC 7303 (section 4.1), but text/xml is still used sometimes. You can assign a specific MIME type to a file with .xml extension depending on how its contents are meant to be interpreted. For instance, an Atom feed is application/atom+xml, but application/xml serves as a valid default.
application/zip. Note, Windows uploads .zip files with the non-standard MIME type application/x-zip-compressed.
